comments.author: @jcany24
comments.text: Nowadays, this small island is just a facade of the former glorious day governed by the Brits. Those were the days, especially now is the so-called " chin-a hong kong " rule by a bunch of low-life criminal pro-commies cops that totally ignored laws & orders but with its own sets of illegal rules since the b.s. national security laws were imposed upon the small islands. Besides, most upper middle well educated professional families have migrants to foreign countries, to the UK, Western European countries & Canada, the US & Singapore. It is now left behind with an empty shell, the multi-millions & billionaires have moved their wealth out of the island by their family's kids. And foreign capital withdrawn from the island etc. since 2020 when COVID 19 started. Worst of all the island economy is facing serious problems due to the strict policies that were imposed recently by the ccp central government to limit the outflow of dollars, through hong kong from mainland chin-a. And to stop the money laundry of the corrupted ccp's members & the business families invest in hong kong stock exchange, real estate properties & registered business to conduct illegal trades to help outflow of money. Note, each banking transaction over USD $50K must report to the central ccp government with approved documents. The $50K cap was meant for the school tuition fees for the family's kid outside the mainland. And the mainland ccp is squeezing every pennies out of the so-called local government coffers. The coming days for hong kong is rather grim as mentioned.

comments.author: @HKSteve88
comments.text:
Whilst the cinematography is beautiful and the narrative good, as a 45+ year resident of Hong Kong I note the following

At 0:58 when the AI generated narration says “this is Hong Kong” that shot is Pudong in Shanghai with Puxi in the distance 

The AI generated narration at7:40 talks about IM Pei’s Bank of China Tower but shows HSBC and Standard Chartered Bank buildings without showing BoC at all. 

10:00. Stanley is not at the eastern end of the island

10:40 “coloon” is Kowloon, pronounced in English as it is spelt Kowloon. Sadly this mistake is repeated throughout the video

12:04. Whilst the narrative is talking about area of Kowloon the video is showing Hong Kong island with its trans and a part of the mid Levels escalator. 

The  numerous mismatch’s between what is being shown versus what is being described with mispronunciation of place names is jarring for a resident

This could have been a great video if the narration had been checked by a resident against the images

replies.author: @TraceTheGlobe
replies.text: We really appreciate this thoughtful feedback. Hearing from a long-time Hong Kong resident gives us a perspective we simply can't get elsewhere. You've highlighted several important mistakes, and we accept those criticisms. Our goal is to share the beauty and story of Hong Kong, and comments like yours help us improve and be more accurate in the future. Thank you for watching and for keeping us accountable.

replies.author: @amt-qg1gx
replies.text: don't understand why foreigners always mention jacky chan when coming to Hong Kong. hk people knew his notorious history.  he is pro ccp and has affairs with many female stars. he also made a female star get pregnant and didn't recognise his daughter.

replies.author: @TraceTheGlobe
replies.text: You're absolutely right to question that. We double-checked the information and realized we got it wrong. Jackie Chan's background is actually the China Drama Academy, not the Hong Kong Academy of Performing Arts. Thanks for helping us keep the content accurate!

comments.author: @HelioTravel
comments.text: This is one of the best Hong Kong travel videos I've watched recently. The breathtaking skyline, bustling streets, local atmosphere, and incredible energy of the city were captured so beautifully. Hong Kong has such a unique character, where tradition and innovation exist side by side. The cinematography and 4K quality made every moment feel immersive. Thank you for taking us on this unforgettable journey through one of Asia's most iconic destinations. I can't wait to visit Hong Kong someday!

replies.author: @HelioTravel
replies.text: @TraceTheGlobe you're welcome

replies.author: @TraceTheGlobe
replies.text: Comments like this inspire us to keep creating! Hong Kong is truly one of Asia's most fascinating destinations, full of contrasts, energy, and unforgettable moments. We're so glad you enjoyed the journey with us, and we sincerely hope you'll be able to explore this incredible city yourself someday. Thanks for watching!
